frame_analyses.description: Title slide 'Chapter 1: Preparation' with equipment tray visible showing FLS trainer setup with graspers, scissors, clip, and endoloop materials
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational setup - no active task performance yet

frame_analyses.description: Illustration of medical team (healthcare provider in white coat discussing with patients). Content transitions from equipment preparation to clinical context
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional/educational segment - demonstrates learning context rather than task performance

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical illustration showing female pelvic anatomy with highlighted reproductive structures; focuses on fallopian tube localization
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content - chapter 5 'Locating Fallopian Tubes' - clinically relevant but not FLS task3 performance

frame_analyses.description: Illustration showing syringe injection technique with cross-sectional anatomical view of abdominal wall layers (fascia, skin, rectus abdominis, peritoneum)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational demonstration of injection technique through anatomical layers - context for procedural learning

frame_analyses.description: Illustration showing hysteroscopic view with surgical instrument (appears to be grasper/clamp) positioned within uterine cavity
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Procedural demonstration - shows surgical approach and instrument positioning

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view showing jumbo clip instrument positioned on tissue (fallopian tube region). Demonstrates clip placement technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgical tool demonstration - shows proper clip application positioning

frame_analyses.description: Anatomical illustration showing post-procedure state with clip applied to fallopian tube. Demonstrates final result of ligating procedure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational outcome illustration - shows successful tissue ligation

frame_analyses.description: Illustration of healthcare team in clinical setting discussing procedure with patient. Final educational frame demonstrating clinical communication
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational wrap-up - demonstrates clinical care context

video_content_summary: This is a comprehensive instructional/educational video on endoloop procedures (task3) that includes FLS equipment setup, anatomical education, procedural demonstrations, and clinical context. The content progresses from equipment preparation through clinical communication but does NOT show an actual trainee performing the FLS task3 ligating loop exercise on the simulator.
confidence_rationale: Video classification is high-confidence instructional content. Multiple indicators: (1) Title slides explicitly labeling chapters ('Chapter 1: Preparation', 'Chapter 5: Locating Fallopian Tubes'); (2) Animated medical illustrations rather than actual simulator performance; (3) Educational narrative structure showing clinical context and anatomy; (4) No visible FLS trainer box, red foam appendage with marked lines, or actual loop deployment sequence; (5) Content designed for educational learning rather than skill assessment.
cannot_determine: Completion time - no actual task performance
Loop placement accuracy - no simulator task shown
Tail cut inside trainer - not demonstrated in this video
scoring_reason: Video is classified as instructional/educational content. Per protocol, instructional videos (containing narration, overlays, title slides, educational illustrations, and clinical context) are NOT scored for FLS task performance. Instead, teaching content is extracted below.

demonstrated_techniques: Equipment preparation and FLS trainer setup
Anatomical identification of reproductive structures (fallopian tubes, uterus, pelvic anatomy)
Proper positioning and approach for surgical intervention
Clip application and tissue ligation technique
Multi-layer approach through abdominal wall (skin, fascia, rectus abdominis, peritoneum)
Proper instrument orientation and grasping technique
Final tissue securing and ligation outcomes
verbal_cues: Chapter-based structure ('Chapter 1: Preparation', 'Chapter 5: Locating Fallopian Tubes') indicating sequential learning progression
Anatomical labels ('Fascia', 'Skin', 'Rectus abdominis', 'Peritoneum') for layer identification
Implied procedural steps shown through sequential illustrations
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding superficial tissue trauma by proper clip application angle
Ensuring complete tissue capture before ligation (demonstrated by clip positioning encompassing entire tube)
Proper depth control through abdominal wall layers
teaching_content.educational_value: High - comprehensive educational resource covering equipment preparation, anatomy, procedural technique, and clinical context. Suitable for trainee orientation and knowledge foundation before hands-on FLS simulator practice.

recommendations: This instructional video is well-suited as pre-task educational material. Trainees should review this content before attempting the FLS task3 simulator to understand anatomical context, procedural goals, and proper technique. However, actual hands-on simulator practice with performance feedback is required for FLS competency assessment.
